name: Police Killing Specialist
notes: Most common ongoing record type. Includes all deaths caused by law enforcement regardless of circumstance. Key sources: DOJ, AP, CourtListener section 1983 cases.

name: Lynching Specialist
notes: Historical record type 1865-1950 primarily. EJI documents 6,500+ cases. Nearly all no_charges. Public spectacle events — crowds, photographs, no prosecution. Some modern lynchings still occur.

name: Massacre Specialist
notes: Mass killings of POC by white mobs or state. Always is_historical=True. Key events: Tulsa 1921, Rosewood 1923, Elaine 1919, Wilmington 1898, MOVE 1985, Osage 1920s. Nearly all no_charges.

name: MMIW Specialist
notes: Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women and People. NEVER is_historical — ongoing crisis. Federal database had only 116 of 5,712 reported cases in 2016. Key sources: Sovereign Bodies Institute, NamUs, tribal records, Indian Country Today.

name: Hate Crime Specialist
notes: Documented racially motivated violence. Must be court-filed or law enforcement confirmed. FBI UCR tracks annually. Underreported — many departments do not submit hate crime data. Key sources: DOJ, CourtListener, FBI UCR.
